zh.Utkinton, Dunham on the Hill, Cheshire,: 英國爾鄧翰 奧 馮 希爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Margareth Hulse於1413年出生在英國爾時，她的父親Thomas de Hulse22歲，母親Alice Hinton18歲。她於1432年與John Troutbeck在英國德比夏爾諾波利結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1457年11月11日在英國英格蘭爾普萊姆斯塔爾享年44歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Margareth Hulse于1413年出生在英国爾时，她的父亲Thomas de Hulse22岁，母亲Alice Hinton18岁。她于1432年与John Troutbeck在英国德比夏爾諾波利结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1457年11月11日在英国英格兰爾普萊姆斯塔爾享年44岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hulse
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Cheshire and Staffordshire): habitational name from Hulse in Witton (Cheshire), from Middle English holes, plural of hole ‘hole, hollow, depression’ (Old English holh). See Hole .
North German (Hülse): topographic name for someone who lived where holly grew, Middle Low German huls, hüls.
American shortened and altered form of Flemish Holsaert (see Hulsart ). Compare Hulce , Huls , and Hults 1.

variants.name: Hulsart
meanings: Americanized form of Flemish Holsaert: unexplained. See also Hulse 3.

variants.name: Hole
meanings: English (southwestern): topographic name for someone who lived in or by a hole or hollow, from Middle English hol(e) (Old English hol, holh, dative hole), or a habitational name from some minor place so named.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of numerous farmsteads, so named from the dative singular or indefinite plural form of Old Norse hóll ‘round hill, mound’.

In some cases possibly also an American shortened form of Flemish Van Hole: habitational name from the common placename Hol(e), meaning ‘hollow, valley’; or a topographic name from the same term or from Middle Dutch hole, heule ‘(low) arched bridge, weir’.

variants.name: Hults
meanings: Altered form of   Hulse  , a surname of Flemish (and also of English and German) origin. Compare   Hultz   1.
In some cases also a variant of   Hultz   2, a surname of German origin.

variants.name: Hulce
meanings: Altered form of   Hulse  , a surname of Flemish (and also of English and German) origin.

nameMeanings.name: Margareth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Margaret
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretha
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Margareth Hulse was born in 1413, in Cheshire, England, her father, Thomas de Hulse, was 22 and her mother, Alice Hinton, was 18. She married John Troutbeck in 1432, in Norbury, Derbyshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 11 November 1457, in Plemstall, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 44.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Margareth Hulse est née en 1413, à Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas de Hulse, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Alice Hinton, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé John Troutbeck en 1432, à Norbury, Derbyshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 11 novembre 1457, à Plemstall, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 44 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Margareth Hulse(이)가 1413년 영국 체실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas de Hulse(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Hinton(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 1432년 영국 더비실르 노르버이에서 John Troutbeck(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1457년 11월 11일 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 플레스탤에서 44세의 나이로 사망했다.

Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Troutbeck в 1432 г. в местности норбури, дирбишир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла 11 ноября 1457 г. в местности племсталл, чешир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 44 лет.
